title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During a game in the dark, a man with a hoarse, nasal voice enters a room, shouting commands and brandishing a flashlight. He fires a revolver twice, and a third shot is fired after a shadow stumbles and collapses. The lights fail, and the room is plunged into darkness. In the ensuing chaos, Miss Blacklock is discovered with blood trickling from her face, which she claims is merely a graze from a bullet near her ear.

Colonel Eastbrook and Edmund investigate the hallway, where they find a body. They also rescue Midge from the dining room, where she had been screaming in terror. As the group gathers candles to provide light, the Colonel examines the deceased, who is dressed in a black hooded cloak, a mask, and gloves. The Colonel observes that the man has golden hair and bloody fingers, suggesting the death may have been a suicide or an accident. When the lights are restored, the Colonel removes the mask and identifies the man as a young individual. Dora Bunner recognizes him as a man from the Medenham Club who had previously asked Miss Blacklock for money to return to Switzerland. Miss Blacklock maintains her composure, ordering Julia to get medical supplies and instructing Patrick to call the police.

In a separate location, Chief Constable George Ridestad receives a report from Inspector Delmont Corrado regarding the incident. Corrado confirms the identity of the deceased as Rudolf Scherz, a Swiss national who worked as a receptionist at the Royal Spa Hotel in Medenham. Corrado plans to investigate the hotel and the mansion.
